description:
This video explains that in 2026, the focus for building functional ai agents will shift from just the model to the 'agent harness'. It details how this harness, crucial for automation, allows llm-powered agents to go beyond text generation and perform real-world actions. For any engineer focused on ai automation and agentic ai, understanding harness engineering is key to deploying effective systems.

----
🚀 DYNAMOUS AI COMMUNITY

Want to learn agentic coding with live daily events and workshops?
Check out Dynamous AI: https://dynamous.ai/?code=646a60
Get 10% off here 👉 https://shorturl.smartcode.diy/dynamous_ai_10_percent_discount

⚡ HOSTINGER — RELIABLE HOSTING FOR YOUR PROJECTS (10% OFF)

Whether you're shipping a portfolio, a side project, n8n flows, or AI agents — I use Hostinger for fast, affordable VPS + web hosting.

Get 10% off here 👉 https://hostinger.com/DIYSMARTCODE

(Affiliate link — costs you nothing, supports the channel.)
----

What you will see in this 2-minute breakdown:
- Why frontier AI models alone cannot ship real agents — the bottleneck is the harness, not the brain
- The core Agent = Model + Harness equation (sports-car engine vs chassis analogy)
- The 9 wired-up blocks: While-Loop Engine, Context Management, Tools + Skills + Registry, Sub-agent Management, Built-in Skills, Session Persistence, System Prompt Assembly, Lifecycle Hooks, Permissions
- Same model, different harness — Claude Code (terminal), Codex CLI (3 permission modes), Cursor (IDE indexing)
- Archon: the open-source harness builder with pre-wired while-loop, context compaction, and event-log persistence

Archon — open-source harness builder: https://archon.diy
Anthropic Claude Code: https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code
OpenAI Codex CLI: https://github.com/openai/codex
Cursor IDE: https://cursor.com

Twelve months from now — better models or better harnesses? Drop your pick below.

fulltitle: Model vs. Harness: The real AI bottleneck
